txt: Brooke, William (Broke), fellow of Oriel Coll. 1503-15, as of the diocese of Canterbury, M.A. (sup. 24 Feb.) 1506-7, proctor, 1511, D.Can.L. (disp. 16 July) 1522, principal or deputy principal of St. Mary Hall 1511-21. See O.H.S., i. 51.
Sparke, William, B.A. of Oxford and M.A. of Cambridge (? 1506); supd. 30 Nov., 1512, for incorporation as M.A.; perhaps fellow Oriel Coll. 4 April, 1503, as of the diocese of Sarum.
Doll, Robert (Dooll or Dole), fellow of Oriel Coll. 1505, M.A. 27 June, 1509, dead by 1512.

Smith, Mathew (Smygth), B.A. 30 June, 1505; fellow Oriel Coll. 27 April, 1506, first principal Brasenose Coll., 1510, M.A. supd. 24 Feb., 1513-14, B.D. disp. 20 Oct., 1519, D.D. supd. 1519; canon of Lincoln, 1508, died 6 Feb., 1547-8, buried in St. Mary's church, Oxford. See Fasti, i. 14; & O.H.S. i. 39.
Lewys, John, chaplain, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1506, until expelled 1510, B.Can.L. sup. 5 May, 1509, B.C.L. 2 July, 1509.
Parker, John, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1506, from Dorset, B.A. 18 Jan., 1508-9.
Richards, Thomas, M.A. 21 June, 1505; fellow Oriel Coll. 1506-11.

Edgeworth, Roger (Egeworth), B.A. 27 Feb., 1507-8, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1508-18, M.A. 6 Feb., 1511-12, B.D. 13 Oct., 1519, D.D. 2 July, 1526, fellow Etor Coll. 1518, canon of Wells 1537, and of Bristol 1542, vicar of St. Cuthbert, Wells, 1543, chancellor of Bath and Wells 1554, buried in Wells Cathedral; his will proved 1 June, 1560. See Foster's Index Eccl.; D.N.B.; Ath. i 315; & Lansdowne MS. 980, f. 327.

Stephens, John (Stephan), B.A. 3 July, 1506, fellow Oriel Coll. 1508-21, M.A. 6 Feb., 1511-12, proctor 1518 (perhaps B.C.L. 7 Nov., 1530, B. and D.D. supd. 5 Feb., 1531-2); possibly rector of Huish Champflower, Somerset, 1523. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Morys, John, B.A. 22 April, 1507, fellow of Oriel Coll. 8 Nov., 1508 M.A. 6 Feb, 1511-12. See Gutch, ii. 11.

Cannings, William (Cannyngs), B.A. (sup. 29 April) 1506, M.A. 4 July, 1513, B.D. (sup. 12 Dec.) 1527, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1509-21, guardian of Schelton and Warwyke chests 1513, vicar of Bishops Lavington 1521. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & O.H.S., i. 39.
Fleming, William (Flemyng), perhaps fellow of Oriel Coll. 1510, B.A. (disp. 16 Jan.), 1510-11, B.Can.L. 3 July, 1514.

Fre(e)man, William, B.A. 1 July, 1510; fellow of Oriel Coll. 31 March, 1512, M.A. 5 March, 1515-16, B.Med. 21 July, 1523, D.Med. 18 Feb., 1527-8; fellow college of physicians 1529, president 1545 and 1546; dead before 1550. See Fasti, i. 75; & Munk's Roll, i. 30.

White, Thomas (Wygt or Wyte), B.A. supd. 19 Oct., 1510; fellow Oriel Coll. 31 March, 1512, M.A. 12 March, 1520-1; perhaps vicar of Heston 1529, and of Hampton-on-Thames, (both) Middlesex, 1532. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rose, William (Rosse), B.A. supd. 22 June, 1510, fellow Oriel Coll. 31 March, 1512, from Hereford, M.A. 5 March, 1515-6, B.Med. June, 1518.
Shoppe, Thomas, B.A. 29 Nov., 1511, fellow Oriel Coll. 1512-22, as of Worcestershire, M.A. 5 March, 1515-16.

Manfield, Thomas (Mawndefylde), fellow of Oriel Coll. 1513-22, from Dorset, B.A. 31 Jan., 1512-13; M.A. of Cambridge, sup. 9 April, 1516, for incorporation; B.D. 17 April, 1526, fellow Eton College 7 Dec., 1521; one of these names B.A. Cambridge 1502-3, M.A. 1506, D.Med. 1510. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.

May, Walter (or Mayo), B.A. 26 Jan., 1511-12; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1513-27, M.A. 13 Dec., 1514, as William, B.D. 1524, D.D. sup. 8 April, 1530; rector of Upton Bishops, co. Hereford, 1543, canon 1533, treasurer of Hereford cathedral 1547. See O.H.S. i. 71; Le Neve, i.; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Knightley, John (or Knygley), s. Sir Richard, of Fawsley; B.A. 4 July, 1512, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1513 to 1522 from Northampton; rector of Stoke Bruern, Northants, 1524, and of Byfield 1539 (dean of the collegiate church of Warwick 1542), until his death in 1549. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Croft, George, B.A. 13 Dec., 1512, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1513-19, from Herefordshire, proctor 1520, perhaps rector of Shepton Mallet, Somerset, 1529 (see Weaver), one of these names chancellor of Chichester 1531. See Dictionary of National Biography, vol. xiii., 115.

Crispin, Richard, B.A. 20 Feb., 1513-14, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1514-27, from Devon, M.A. 14 July, 1519, junior proctor 1522, B.D. (sup. 12 Dec.) 1527, D.D. (sup. 5 Feb.) 1531-2, canon of Exeter 1541. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; & O.H.S., i. 88.
Upton, William, fellow Oriel Coll. 1516, from diocese of Chichester, M.A. 14 July, 1519; rector of Etchingham, Sussex; will at Oxford proved 11 March, 1528. See O.H.S. i. 110.
Slade, Thomas, B.A. supd. 13 Feb., 1513-14, fellow Oriel Coll. 1516-21, from Somerset, M.A. 14 July, 1519; rector of West Coker, Somerset, 1524. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Lorgan, Richard (Lorgam or Lorgon), fellow of Oriel Coll. 1519, from Pembrokeshire, principal of St. Mary Hall 1521-30, M.A. 23 July, 1523, proctor 1527, B.D. 31 Jan., 1531-2, D.D. 9 July, 1534, reader of divinity at Magdalen Coll., admon. at Oxford 18 May, 1536. See Fasti, i. 99; & O.H.S. i. 128.
Throckmorton, John (Throgemorton or Frogmerton), fellow of Oriel Coll. 1519 from Gloucestershire, M.A. 23 July, 1523; admon. at Oxford 4 May, 1529.

Rixman, John, fellow Oriel Coll. 1521, from Devon, B.A. 7 Feb., 1520-1, M.A. 13 July, 1525, B.D. supd. — May, 1532, principal of St. Mary Hall 1532-7, rector of Trevalga, Cornwall, 1530-38, rector of St. Clement Danes, Middlesex, 1540, canon of Exeter 1544, vicar of Berry Pomeroy, Devon, 1549, of Brixham, with Churston Ferrers, 1554, and precentor of Exeter cathedral 1555, canon of Bristol 1554, archdeacon of Cornwall 1554; died Dec., 1557. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Charde, Robert, B.A. 1 Dec., 1520; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1521, from Dorset, M.A. 13 July, 1525, vicar of Bradpole, Dorset, 1542, preb. of Collegiate Church of St. Peter's, in Warwick, 1543. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Ashe, Thomas (Asche), B.A. 26 Oct., 1519, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1521, from Somerset. See O.H.S., i.

Jury, Thomas, B.A. 26 Jan., 1519-20; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1522, from Wilts, M.A. 27 June, 1526, rector of Bishopstrow, Wilts, 1532-41, and perhaps rector of Crocombe, Somerset, 1541, until his death in 1575. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rishton, Alexander (Ryston, Rysthon, or Rushton), B.A. 6 Feb., 1520-1; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1522, from co. Lancaster, M.A. 27 June, 1526, B.D. supd. Dec., 1536; admon. at Oxford 2 Oct., 1537.

Haynes, William (Heynys, or Heyne), B.A. 27 June, 1522; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1524, from Somerset, M.A. 14 Feb., 1527-8, B.D. 15 Feb., 1537-8, provost 1540-50, canon of Christ Church 1547; fellow of Eton Coll. 27 Jan., 1533; died 8 July, 1550. See Fasti, i. 105.

Griffith, John (Gryffethe), B.A. 14 Feb., 1521-2; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1524, from Pembroke; M.A. 14 Feb., 1527-8, perhaps B.D. 18 Feb., 1541-2, canon of St. Asaph 1536, vicar of Llanrwst, co. Denbigh, 1537, sinecure rector of Llanbrynmair. See Thomas, 565.
Woodhall, John (Woodwall or Wodall), B.A. 27 June, 1523; fellow Oriel Coll. 1524, from Worcester, M.A. 14 Feb., 1527-8.
Mo(u)lton, Robert, B.A. 21 Feb., 1524-5. fellow of Oriel Coll. 26 Oct., 1525, from Northants; admon. (as Multon) at Oxford 16 Dec., 1528.
Molton, Robert, B.A. 21 Feb., 1524-5; fellow of Oriel Coll. 26 Oct., 1525, from Northants; admon. (as Multon) at Oxford 16 Dec., 1528.
Multon, Robert (Molton), B.A. 21 Feb., 1524-5, fellow of Oriel Coll. 26 Oct., 1525, from Northants; admon. at Oxford 16 Dec., 1528.
James, Robert (Jamys), B.A. 28 Nov., 1524; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1525, from Somerset, M.A. 11 March, 1528-9.
Winter, Roger, B.A. supd. 11 Nov., 1522, fellow Oriel Coll. 1525, from Salop, M.A. 11 March, 1528-9.

Courthope, Thomas (Curtoppe), B.A. of Cambridge, incorporated 17 July, 1525, M.A. 31 Jan., 1531-2, fellow Oriel Coll. 6 May, 1527, from Kent, rector of Buckland, Kent, 1545, of Canterbury St. Mary Bredman 1550, and of Woodchurch 1551. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Pye, William, B.A. 28 Jan., 1528-9; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1529-44 from Suffolk, M.A. supd. May, 1533, proctor 1536, 1542, 1543, B.Med. sup. May, 1534, principal of St. Mary Hall 1537-43, B.D. 8 Feb., 1543-4, D.D. sup. March, 1543-4, rector of Westley 1542, and of Risby, Suffolk, 1547, archdeacon of Berks 1545, with rectory of North Moreton, Berks, 1547, canon of Lichfield 1550, and of Wells 1553, dean of Chichester 1553, rector of Chedzoy, Somerset, 1554, and canon of Westminster 1554, master of Chichester hospital 1555, and canon of Windsor Jan., 1556-7, until his death before Sept. following. See Ath. i. 247; & Lansdowne MS. 980, f. 275.

Carden, Thomas, B.A. 28 Jan., 1528-9; fellow Oriel Coll. 1529 to 1537, from Kent, as 'Cardyne,' M.A. (sup. May) 1533, one of these names rector of Dickleburgh, Norfolk, 1551. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Hughes, Richard (Hewys or Huys), B.A. (sup. 18 May), 1528, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1529-34 from Oxfordshire, M.A. (sup. in May), 1533, "sup. July, 1534, for leave to practice medicine."
Smithe, John (Smythe), B.A. sup. 26 Jan., 1528-9, junior fellow Oriel Coll. 1530, M.A. supd. May, 1533, proctor 1546, provost 1550-64, (B.D.), Margaret professor of divinity 1554-61.
Adams, John (Adamys), B.A. 16 May, 1528, fellow of Oriel Coll. 9 June, 1531, from Gloucestershire, in diocese of Hereford, M.A. 3 Feb., 1532-3 (as Thomas). See Le Neve, iii. 457; & O.H.S., i.
Robarts, Thomas, B.A. 18 July, 1530, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1531-41, from Gloucestershire, M.A. 9 July, 1535; perhaps vicar of Llanynis, co. Denbigh, 1541. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Cocks, William (Cockys or Cockis), B.A. 27 Feb., 1532-3, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1533 (from Gloucestershire in diocese of Worcester), M.A. 18 June, 1537.
Hughes, William (Huys), secular chaplain, B.Can.L. 8 July, 1532, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1533-39, from Berks.

Colling, William (Colyng or Colyn), B.A. 14 Feb., 1533-4, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1534, M.A. 15 Feb., 1537-8, perhaps B.D., D.D. (sup.) 1552, one of these names rector of Little Fakenham, Suffolk, 1545, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Durston, John, B.A. 8 July, 1532, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1534-54, M.A. 15 Feb., 1537-8, perhaps canon of Chichester 1554, and fellow of Eton 1555. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Fre(e)man, Richard, B.A. 19 Dec., 1534. Fellow of Oriel Coll. 1536, from Northants, M.A. 27 March, 1539; probably admon. at Oxford 28 May, 1546.
Albons, Antony, B.A. 27 Feb., 1532-3, M.A. 27 March, 1539, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1536, from Herefordshire, principal of St. Mary Hall 1543-6.

Bentham, Laurence (Bentam), B.A. 29 May, 1536, M.A. 22 March, 1542-3; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1537-48, from Somerset, vicar of Ellingham, Hants, 1548, rector of Pylle, Somerset, 1554. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Gore, John, B.A. 15 Feb., 1533-4; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1537-46, from Wilts, M.A. 22 March, 1541-2; "late esquire bedel of law;" will at Oxford proved 21 Dec., 1595.
Rumsey, John, B.A. 20 Jan., 1536-7; fellow Oriel Coll. 1537, from Somerset, M.A. 22 March, 1542, rector of Chalcombe, Somerset, 1554. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Phillips, Morgan (Phyllyppes), B.A. 18 Feb., 1537-8, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1538-54, M.A. 22 March, 1541-2; "commonly called Morgan the sophister," principal of St. Mary Hall 1546-50, then B.D., rector of Cuddington, Oxon, 1543, vicar of St. Winnock, co. Pembroke, 1549, chantor of St. David's cathedral 1554, left his preferments, friends and country on the accession of Q. Elizabeth, died at Douay, admon. 15 Feb., 1577. See Ath. i. 432; Fasti, i. 105; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Crispin, Edmund, B.A. 20 Jan., 1536-7, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1538-45, from Devon, M.A. 22 March, 1541-2, B.Med. (sup. July) 1543, D.Med. (sup.) 1547-8, proctor 1547, perhaps beneficed in Devon. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Norfolk, William (Northfolk), B.A. 12 Jan., 1544-5; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1546-56, from diocese of York, principal of St. Mary Hall 1550-3, proctor 1555, prebendary of Worcester 1558, but deprived same year, rector of Hanbury, co. Worcester, 1559. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Howell, David, B.A. 11 Dec., 1544, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1546-55, from diocese of Llandaff; one of these names beneficed in Pembrokeshire. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wood, William (Wodd), B.A. 12 Jan., 1544-5; fellow Oriel Coll. 1546, from diocese of Worcester; perhaps M.A. and principal of St. Mary Hall 1553-6.

Row, John (Roo), B.A. supd. Michaelmas term 1546, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1547-65, from diocese of Exeter, M.A. sup. 1549; perhaps rector of Swanswick, Bath; perhaps principal of St. Mary Hall about 1560-5 (as Raw). See Foster's Index Eccl.
Edwardes, John, B.A. (sup. Michs. Term), 1546, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1547, M.A. (sup.) 1549, B.Med. and admitted to practice 4 July, 1558. Will at Oxford proved 22 Feb., 1588.
Story, George, B.A. supd. 1548, fellow Oriel Coll. 1549, from co. Durham, M.A. 14 July, 1553, incorporated at Cambridge 1555; rector of Great Weldon, Northants, 1553. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Shewell, Richard (Scewall), B.A. supd. 1548, fellow Oriel Coll. 1549, from Derbyshire, M.A. supd. June, 1553.
Ewer, John, B.A. (sup.) 1548, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1549-54, from Herefordshire.
Michell, Henry, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1550-9, from York; B.A. sup. Oct., 1545, M.A. 13 July, 1554; perhaps rector of Whatcott, co. Warwick, 1573. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Hughes, Robert (Hewys, Hewes, or Huys), B.A.; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1551-9, from diocese of St. Asaph, M.A. 26, July, 1555, cursal canon of St. Asaph 1560, rector of Llanycil, co. Merioneth, 1561, proctor in convocation 1562. See Thomas, 724; & Foster's Index Eccl.

White, Peter, (B.A.), fellow Oriel Coll. 1551, from Waterford, M.A. 26 July, 1555; "the happy schoolmaster of Munster," and dean of Waterford 1566-70. See Ath. i. 565; Cotton, i. 138, and ii. 414; & Fasti, i. 147.
Hemming, John, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1551; from Worcestershire, B.A.; M.A. 26 July, 1555.
Griffith, Thomas (Griphith), B.A. Dec., 1552, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1553, from Kent, in diocese of Canterbury, M.A. (sup. 21 June), 1557; sup. June, 1562, for B.Med. and leave to practice.
Webber, Francis, B.A. — Dec., 1552; fellow Oriel Coll. 1554, from Somerset, M.A. 1 July, 1557, elected dean of his college 1565, refused the oath and was expelled.

Belly, John (Bellay), B.A. 11 Feb., 1554-5, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1556, M.A., 17 March, 1558-9, B.C.L. and D.C.L. 7 July, 1567, provost 1566-73, incorp. at Cambridge 1574 (son of John Belly, or Bellay, of Haselbury, Somerset), canon of Lincoln 1574, a master in Chancery and chancellor of the diocese of Lincoln, died at Great Paxton 1608. See Cooper, ii. 481.
Herniman, John, B.A. 11 Feb., 1554-5, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1556-60, M.A. 17 March, 1558-9; perhaps rector of Barcombe, Sussex, 1580. See Gutch, ii. 136, 147.

Sherley, (Sir) Thomas (Shirley, or Sharley), B.A. 23 Feb., 1556-7, fellow Oriel Coll. 1557; perhaps of Wiston, Sussex, student of Gray's Inn 1559 (s. William), knighted 12 Aug., 1573, M.P. Sussex 1572-83, 1584-5, 1593, Steyning 1601; died Oct., 1612; father of the next. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Edgworth, Richard, B.A. 23 Feb., 1556-7, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1557, M.A. 12 May, 1563.
Brechin, Alexander (or Brekin); B.A. of St. Andrew's in Scotland, incorp. 28 June, 1560; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1560-78 from diocese of Durham, M.A. 22 March, 1564-5.

Jones, William (Jonys or Johns), B.A. 25 Oct., 1559; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1561, from co. Carnarvon, M.A. 12 May, 1563; perhaps of "Merton Coll. 1564, M.A.," B.C.L. 26 April, 1570, D.C.L. (sup. Feb.), 1570-1, possibly a student of Middle Temple 1565, and perhaps of Castelmarch, M.P. Beaumaris 1597-8, 1604-11, 1614, and co. Carnarvon 1601. See Lansd. MS. 981, f. 146; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Hurlock, John (Horlock), B.A. 13 Feb., 1560-1, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1561, from Middlesex, M.A. 22 March, 1564-5, B.D. 1573 (sup. 26 Apl. 1571), rector of Exton, Hants, 1571, vicar of Shalfleete, Isle of Wight, 1572, rector of North Waltham 1573, and vicar of Southampton St. Cross, or Holy Rood, 1573. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Horlocke, John, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1561, sup. for B.D. 26 April, 1571; principal of St. Mary Hall 1565-70. See O.H.S. xii. 11.

Hill, Robert, B.A. 19 Nov., 1561; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1562-72, from Salop, in diocese of Lichfield, M.A. 5 July, 1566, B.D. 21 Jan., 1580-1; one of these names rector of Tredington, co. Worcester, 1572-1606, and of Barcheston, co. Warwick, 1572-1606, and archdeacon of Gloucester 1602-6. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Cogan, Thomas (Coghan or Coggin), B.A. 14 Jan., 1562-3, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1563-75, from Somerset, M.A. 5 July, 1566, B.Med. and licenced to practice 31 March, 1574, canon of Exeter 1567, chief master of Manchester Collegiate School, buried in St. Anne's 10 June, 1607. See Palatine Note Book, iii. 77-84; Ath., ii. 19; & D.N.B.

Philipson, Thomas, B.A. 16 Feb., 1562-3; fellow Oriel Coll. 1563, from diocese of Lincoln; M.A. 5 July, 1566 (incorp. at Cambridge 1573), B.C.L. sup. 14 Dec., 1579; principal of St. Mary Hall between 1570-87; will at Oxford proved 26 Jan., 1588. See Ath. ii. 300; & Cooper, ii. 86.
Beddoe, John (Bedow or Bedoe), B. Gramm., 8 July, 1558, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1563, from diocese of St. David's, rector of Llanvihangel Penbedw, co. Pembroke, 1568. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Powell, Thomas, B.A. 26 Feb., 1564-5; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1565, from Herefordshire, M.A. 30 June, 1569, prelector of natural philosophy 1570; inventory at Oxford 6 June, 1575.
Hindmarsh, Michael (Hindmer or Heinmer), B.A. 17 Jan., 1564-5; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1565, from Westmorland.

Reynolds, Richard (Rainolds or Reinolds), B.A. 5 Feb., 1564-5. fellow Oriel Coll. 1566, M.A. 26 June, 1570 (incorporated at Cambridge 1575), B.D. 9 May, 1577; perhaps rector of Stapleford Abbots 1566, of Lambourne 1569, and vicar of West Thurrock (all Essex) 1578. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Evans, Lewis, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1566-7, from co. Brecon, B.A. 21 Feb., 1565-6, M.A. 26 June, 1570; one of these names vicar of Bibury, co. Gloucester, 1566, canon of Wells, 1566, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Cooke, Thomas, B.A. 31 May, 1568, M.A. 6 July, 1573, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1569-82, from the diocese of Sarum, perhaps vicar of East Brent, Somerset, 1582, until death in 1610. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Ware, George, B.A. 26 Jan., 1558-9, fellow Oriel Coll. 1569.

Kir(k)by, Richard, B.A. Cambridge 1567-8; incorporated 10 Feb., 1569-70; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1570-75, from Middlesex, M.A. 27 June, 1573, rector of West Bergholt 1575, vicar of White Notley 1588, and of Easter-Alta (all Essex), 1597. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Osmonde, R, of Oriel Coll. in or before 1572; one John Osmond an original scholar of Jesus Coll. 1571. See Gutch, i. 569.
Lewes, Edward, B.A. 7 April, 1571, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1572, M.A. 26 June, 1574, having been allowed to count all terms and exercises at Cambridge. See O.H.S. x. 263.
Bonde, William, B.A. 19 June, 1574; fellow of Oriel Coll. 1575, M.A. 17 Feb., 1578-9, one of these names vicar of Stockland, Dorset, 1585. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Bennet, Edward (Benet), B.A. 31 March, 1574, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1575. See O.H.S., xii. 41.

Eglesfeilde, Anthony, of Oriel Coll.; B.A. (sup. 18 June), 1574, M.A. 1 July, 1577, fellow of Queen's Coll. 1577, rector of Charlton on Otmoor, Oxon, 1578, of Leigh Delamere, Wilts, 1580, of Draycot Cerne, Wilts, 1583, of Stanton St. Quinton, Wilts, 1586, vicar of Chewton Mendip, Somerset, 1589, rector of Streat with Walton, Somerset, 1608, until his death in 1635. See Foster's Index Eccl.; O.H.S. xii. 45.

Webbe, William, of Wilts, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18; perhaps student of Lincoln's Inn 1589, knighted 14 March, 1603-4. See Betham's Baronetage, ii. p. 19; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Nelson, William, fellow Oriel Coll. 1577, B.A. 14 Dec., 1577, M.A. 12 April, 1581. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Medlycot, Thomas (Midlecot), of Wilts, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 16.
Pope, Robert, of Dorset, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 18.
Webbe, John, of Wilts, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 17.
Tye, James, of Devon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date 20 Dec., 1577, aged 12.

Edwardes, John, of Oxon, gen. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 13; perhaps of the Inner Temple 1587, as of Oxford, and late of Clement's Inn, gen. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Paynter, William, of London, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 14 (Payter in O.H.S.).
Whitehande, Bartholomew, of London, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date 3 Aug., 1578, aged 17.
Stafforde, Edward, of co. Stafford, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1580, aged 15; student of Gray's Inn 1589, as son and heir of Sir Robert. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Torbock, Edward, of co. Lancaster, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1580, aged 17; son of Sir Edward, of Torbock, who was knighted 1 Nov., 1606, and brother of Thomas.
Gatacre, William (Catagree), of Salop, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1580, aged 13; of Gatacre (s. Francis), buried at Claverly 27 April, 1615.
Torbock, Thomas, of co. Lancaster, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1580, aged 15; brother of Edward.
Sheldon, William, of co. Stafford, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1580, aged 15.
Leveson, Edward, of co, Stafford, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1580, aged 15.
Nelson, John, of co. Lancaster, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1580, aged 17.
Waringe, John, of co. Warwick, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1580, aged 17.
Lane, William, of Berks, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1580, aged 15.
White, Thomas, of Hants, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. entry under date circa 1580, aged 16.

Clavell, (Sir) William, of Dorset. Oriel Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 18 (as gent.); fellow 1583, B.A. 6 Feb., 1583-4, M.A. 4 May, 1587, of the Middle Temple 1588 (as son of John, of Barneston, Purbeck Island, Dorset), of Barneston and afterwards of Smedmore, knight banneret, baptized 22 July, 1568, made his will 15 March, 1643, proved 13 March, 1644. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Speccott, (Sir) John, of Devon, arm. fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 20; of Thornbury, student of Middle Temple 1582 (as son and heir of Humphrey, of Thornbury, Devon), sheriff of Cornwall 1622, knighted 2 June, 1604, M.P. St. Mawes 1604-11; died in 1641, father of the next and of Peter. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Welshe, James, of Somerset, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 23 Nov., 1581, aged 18, B.A. 17 Feb., 1584-5; M.A. from St. Mary Hall 20 Nov., 1593; rector of Compton Bishop, Somerset, 1592. See Index Eccl.
Pressie, Thomas (Presie), of Wilts, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 23 Nov., 1581, aged 14; student of Inner Temple 1589, as of Bishopstone, Wilts, gent. (as Precy). See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Stanford, Edward, of co. Stafford, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, this entry erased; one of these names rector of Checkley, co. Stafford, 1579. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Forward, John, Oriel Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 20, "serviens coll."; B.A. 22 March, 1582-3, rector of Playden, Sussex, 1588. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Holmes, George (Homes), of Oxon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 21; B.A. 10 July, 1582, as Homes; query father of Nathaniel.
Franke, Thomas, of Salop, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 20; sup. for B.A. 30 April, 1584. See O.H.S. xii. 125.
Walker, Robert, of Cumberland, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 Feb., 1581-2, aged 24, B.A. 14 June, 1583, M.A. 5 July, 1588.
Clinckarde, Edmond, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 20; called Clincaudd in Mat. Reg.
Smithe, John (Smythe), of co. Gloucester. Oriel Coll., matric. 23 Nov., 1581, aged 20, "servius."
Whitehead, Robert (Whiteyde), of co. Durham, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 18.
Sheldon, Thomas, of co. Worcester, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 23 Nov., 1581, aged 15.
Brodrip, Thomas, of Somerset, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 23 Nov., 1581, aged 14.
Woodleff, Edmond, of Bucks, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 23 Nov., 1581, aged 16.
Mason, Richard, of Salop, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 23 Nov., 1581, aged 17.
Steade, Thomas, of Yorks, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 24 Nov., 1581, aged 18.
Whitton, Henry, of Kent, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 23 Nov., 1581, aged 18.
Bonner, William, of Oxon. Oriel Coll., matric. 23 Nov., 1581, aged 48.

Rutter, John, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1582-3, aged 16; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 30 March, 1587; "scriptor et poeta," rector of Dorsington, co. Gloucester, 1593; father of Ferriman and Timothy. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Finche, John, of Kent, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1582, aged 14; student of Gray's Inn 1588, as son and heir of Clement of Milton-next-Sittingbourne, Kent; perhaps brother of Antony and Henry 1586, and of Thomas 1582. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Tot(h)ill, Christopher, of co. Lancaster, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 July, 1582, aged 18, B.A. 4 Feb., 1585-6, M.A. 10 Feb., 1588-9 (as Toothill).
Finche, Thomas, of Kent, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1582, aged 15; brother of Anthony and Henry 1586, and of John 1582.
Bulkeley, William (Buckeley), of Salop, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 May, 1582, aged 15.
Nicols, Thomas, of co. Warwick, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 March, 1582-3, aged 16.
Grantham, James, of Cheshire, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 25 Jan., 1582-3, aged 21.
Baron, Henry, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 March, 1582-3.

Mason, Francis, of Durham, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 10 May, 1583, aged 17; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 27 Jan., 1586-7; fellow Merton Coll. 1586, M.A. 4 July, 1590, licenced to preach 30 June, 1597, B.D. 7 July, 1597, "Vindex Ecclesiae Anglicanae," rector of Sudborne with chapelry of Orford, Suffolk, 1599, chaplain to James I., archdeacon of Norfolk 1614, until his death Dec., 1621; buried in the chancel of Orford church; brother of John 1591. See Ath. ii. 305; Foster's Index Eccl.; & O.H.S. iv. 274.
Hathwat, Thomas, of Cumberland, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 15 Nov., 1583, aged 19; B.A. 16 Feb., 1587-8, M.A. 6 July, 1593 (as Heathwat), rector of Buttermere, Wilts, 1594. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Brycott, John (Bryckett), of Northants, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 26 April, 1583, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1593, as of Morten, Northants. See Foster's Gray's Inn Admissions.
Beale, Thomas, of co. Hereford, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 31 May, 1583, aged 14; a student of the Inner Temple 1587, as of Stanton, co. Hereford. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Massye, James, of co. Lancaster, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1583, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1595, as of Rixton, co. Lancaster. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Wandesford, Christopher, of Yorks, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 13 Dec., 1583, aged 17; probably 4s. Christopher, of Hipswell, Yorks. See Foster's Yorkshire Visitations.
Starkey, Peter, of Cheshire, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1583, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1595. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Lewes, David, fellow of Oriel Coll. 1583, B.A. 5 June, 1583; perhaps rector of Llandevalley, co. Brecon, 1584. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Best, John, of Kent, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1583, aged 14; B.A. (sup. 20 Oct.) 1587. See also William 1589.
Holt, Robert, of co. Lancaster, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. March, 1583-4, aged 19; brother of William 1586.
Pratten, George, of Somerset, paup. schol. Oriel Coll., matric. 26 April, 1583, aged 15.
Smithe, Richard (Smythe), of Hants, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 21 June, 1583, aged 21.
Tovey, William (Tovye), of Wilts, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1583, aged 16.
Raile, Rowland, of co. Derby, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 21 Feb., 1583-4, aged 16.
Sheldon, Francis, of co. Worcester, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 March, 1584-5, aged 16; perhaps student Gray's Inn 1580. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Robins, Richard, of Oxon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 8 May, 1584, aged 17, B.A. supd. 10 Feb. 1587-8.
Fuleshurst, George, of co. Warwick, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 22 Jan., 1584-5, aged 16.
Sheldon, John, of co. Worcester, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 March, 1584-5, aged 15.

Freke, William, of Dorset, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1594, as 4s. of Robert, late of Iwerne Courtney, Dorset, esq., baptised there 3 Oct., 1568, removed into Ireland; father of Arthur 1623. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Beeston, Peter, of Cheshire, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 18 June, 1585, aged 18; a student of the Middle Temple 1591, as late of New Inn, gent., 3rd son of George, of Beeston, Cheshire, knight. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Cockram, Humfrey (Cokram), of Devon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 14; of Cullompton (son of Philip), buried there 22 March, 1636, brother of Martin.
Wakeline, Alban, of Northants, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 15, student of Gray's Inn 1586 as of Eydon, Northants. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Harewell, (Sir) Edmund, of co. Worcester. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 18 (as arm.), of Besford (s. Edmund); father of the next-named.
Stephens, Edmund (Steavenes), of Oxon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. entry 2 July, 1585, aged 14.
Berrier, Gregory, of Oxon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. in 1585, aged 16; B.A. 5 Feb., 1590-1.
Tipprisse, Thomas, of Berks, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 19. See Torlesse.
Smith, Henry (Smyth), of co. Lancaster, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 25 June, 1585, aged 16.
Scarisbricke, Edward, of co. Lancaster, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 25 June, 1585, aged 16.
Hunter, Robert, of co. Lancaster, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 25 June, 1585, aged 20.
Cole, Richard, of co. Gloucester, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 18.
Taylor, Christopher, of Yorks, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 20.
Edgebaston, Thomas, of Devon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 13.
Woodleife, Thomas, of Bucks, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 15.
Piggott, Gervase, of Notts, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 17.
Dabbes, Thomas, of Kent, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 18 June, 1585, aged 14.
Ingram, William, of Oxon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 July, 1585, aged 16.

Fetherstonhalgh, Ralph, of co. Durham, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 15 Jan., 1586-7, aged 13, B.A. 9 July, 1590; student of Gray's Inn 1591, (as of Stanhope, co. Durham,) perhaps M.P. Morpeth (Feb.) 1621 to 1622. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Greene, James, of co. Stafford, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 July, 1586, aged 19 (subs. as John); B.A. from St. Mary Hall 14 Nov., 1589; M.A. from Oriel Coll. 9 July, 1592, rector of Enville, co. Stafford, 1594. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Morrell, John, of co. Gloucester, "gen. cond." Oriel Coll., matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 17; B.A. 16 Dec., 1589, vicar of Broadway, co. Worcester, 1591, rector of Hinton-on-the-Green, co. Gloucester, 1596. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Finch, Henry, of Kent, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 July, 1586, aged 17; erroneously identified in Ath. ii. 387; brother of Anthony same date, and perhaps of John and Thomas 1582.
Finch, Anthony, of Kent, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 July, 1586, aged 14, called Fintche in Mat. Reg.; brother of Henry same date, and perhaps of John and Thomas 1582.
Jackson, John, of co. Lincoln, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 18; one of these names vicar of Horncastle, co. Lincoln, 1593. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Maire, Christopher (Mayer), of co. Durham, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 22 April, 1586, aged 17 (s. Christopher); buried at Monk Hesledon, co. Durham, 19 Dec., 1603.
Harris, Thomas (Harrys), of co. Lincoln, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 17 June, 1586, aged 15; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 7 Feb., 1589-90.
Bullessey, William, of York, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 18; Bollacsie in Mat. Reg.
Engeham, Henry, of Kent, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Nov., 1586, aged 16, B.A. 1 July, 1590.
Mayland, Christopher, of co. Durham, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 15 Jan., 1586-7, aged 19.
Cooke, William, of Northants, 'cler. fil.' Oriel Coll., matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 11.
Tilston, Thomas (Tylston), of Salop, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 8 July, 1586, aged 23.

Lister, (Sir) Mathew, of Yorks, gen. fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 23 Feb., 1587-8, aged 17; B.A. 5 Feb., 1590-1, fellow, M.A. 2 July, 1595; D.Med. of Basle University, incorporated 15 May, 1605, and at Cambridge 1608; of Burwell, co. Lincoln (s. William, of Thornton in Craven, Yorks), fellow college of physicians 1607, physician to Queen Anne, consort of James I., physician in ordinary to Charles I., and knighted at Oatlands 11 Oct., 1636; died Dec., 1656, aged 92; buried at Burwell. See Fasti, i. 307; & Munk's Roll, i. 123.
Perkins, Abel, of Dorset, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1587, aged 17 (as Parkins), B.A. 8 June, 1591, vicar of Affpuddle, Dorset, 1614. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Berriman, Samuel (Beriman), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 March, 1587-8, aged 17; B.A. 5 Feb., 1591-2.
Russell, Thomas, of Sussex, pleb. Oriel Coll, matric. 1 Dec., 1587, aged 18.

Carrill, (Sir) Thomas (Caryll), of Sussex. Oriel Coll., matric. 8 June, 1588, aged 15 (as arm.); of Bentons in Shipley, Sussex (son of Sir Edward), father and son seem to have been knighted on the same day, viz., 11 May, 1603, baptized at Warnham 12 April, 1571, died 30 Jan., 1616-17, half-brother of Richard 1605.

Fetherstonhalgh, Francis, of co. Durham, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 July, 1588, aged 13; student of Gray's Inn 1591 (as of Stanhope, co. Durham, gent.); one of these names M.P. New Romney 1621-2, and 1624-5. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Kinsman, George (Kinesman), of Northants, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 March, 1588-9, aged 14; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1614, as 4th son of William, late of Hoddington, Northants, esq., deceased. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Robinsonn, George, of co. Durham, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 July, 1588, aged 17, B.A. 3 Feb. 1591-2, M.A. 18 Nov., 1594; one of these names rector of Rousham, Oxon, 1598, and father of George 1634.
Washington, Christopher, of Northants, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 6 Dec., 1588, aged 15, B.A. 22 Feb., 1594-5 (s. of Robert, of Sulgrave); brother of William same date.
Washington, William, of Northants, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. entry 6 Dec., 1588 aged 11 (s. Robert, of Sulgrave), brother of Christopher.
Ayraye, Anthony, of co. Durham, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 & 12 July, 1588, aged 17; B.A. 3 Feb., 1591-2.
Beache, John, of Sussex, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 July, 1588, aged 18.
Gin, George, of London, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 6 Dec., 1588, aged 15.
Bright, John, of Oxon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 6 Aug., 1588, aged 30.

Tolson, John, of Cumberland, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. entry 30 Jan., 1589-90, aged 14, B.A. 6 Dec., 1593, M.A. 23 Feb., 1598-9, proctor 1607, B.D. 25 June, 1611, D.D. 21 March, 1621-2, provost of his college 1621-44, pro-vice-chancellor Sept., 1642—Feb., 1642-3, and vice-chancellor 7 Feb., 1642-3 (s. Henry, of Bridekirk); vicar of Nottingham St. Mary 1616, rector of Hampton Poyle, Oxon, 1619, canon of St. Paul's 1632, and rector of Marston Sicca, co. Gloucester, 1634; died 10 or 16 Dec., 1644; will at Oxford proved 29 Dec., 1644. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Owen, Evan, of co. Merioneth, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 May, 1589, aged 15; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 15 July, 1603 (as Owens); vicar of Beguildy, co. Radnor, 1605; father of Elias 1634. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Owen, John, of co. Merioneth, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 May, 1589, aged 17; student of Inner Temple 1594, as of Llanfihangel, co. Merioneth, gent. (son of John, M.P. Merioneth). See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Brewer, Anthony (Bruer), of Wilts, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 May, 1589, aged 16; B.A. 9 Feb., 1593-4, M.A. 6 July, 1598, will proved (at Oxford) 3 May, 1602, as Bruer.
Coney, George (Conye), of Sussex, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 May, 1589, aged 19, called Calvet' in Mat. Reg.
Harison, Joseph, of Sussex, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 18 July, 1589, aged 17; B.A. 6 July, 1594.
Best, William, of Kent, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 May, 1589, aged 17. See also John 1583.
Beake, Simon, of Kent, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 27 Feb., 1589-90, aged 17.

Eales, William, of co. Warwick, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1590. aged 17; B.A. 22 Oct., 1595, M.A. 21 June, 1599, rector of Hatford, Berks, 1602; licenced 26 Feb., 1619-20, to marry Isabell, widow of William Cotterell. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster, & Foster's Index Eccl.
Gresbrooke, Richard, of Notts, pleb., Oriel Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1590, aged 18; B.A. 30 Oct., 1594, M.A. 6 July, 1598 (then in orders), vicar of Bethersden, Kent, 1598. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Dauntesey, John, of co. Lancaster, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1590, aged 18; B.A. from St. John's Coll. 25 Feb., 1597-8. See William same date.
Ja(u)ncey, Christopher, of co. Worcester, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1590, aged 19; B.A. from Christ Church 5 June, 1594.
Dauntesey, William, of co. Lancaster, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1590, aged 19. See John same date.
Poyner, Anthony, of co. Gloucester, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1590, aged 15.
Osborn, John, of London, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 23 Oct., 1590, aged 15.

Lloyd, Richard, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18 (as Floyd); B.A. 16 Dec., 1595; M.A. from Lincoln Coll. 6 July, 1598, B.D. 18 March, 1605-6, D.D. 1617, rector of Llangwm, co. Pembroke, 1601, vicar of Machynlleth, co. Montgomery, 1602, rector of Llanwchyllyn 1603, of Trawsfynydd, co. Merioneth, 1606, a cursal canon of St. Asaph 1611, vicar of Llangerniew 1609, of Abergele 1611, of Gresford 1613, of Ruabon 1615-42, and rector of Marchwiel (all co. Denbigh), 1615, sinecure rector of Llansannan (2nd portion) 1617, and of Ysecifiog, Flints, 1621; died at Ruabon about 1642; father of Rice 1632, of Samuel 1626, and of Humphrey 1628. See Fasti, i. 373; Thomas, St. Asaph; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Watts, Edward, of Northants, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 16, B.A. 30 April, 1594; baptised at Blakesley 8 June, 1574, bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1602 (as son and heir of William, of Blakesley, Northants); buried in the Temple church 28 Oct., 1647; brother of Montague. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Cockram, Martin, of Devon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 16; B.A. 29 Jan., 1596-7, M.A. 10 July, 1601 (son of Philip, of Cullompton), vicar of Holcombe Rogus, Devon, 1608-13. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Oxonbregge, Goddard, of Hants, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 17; student of Inner Temple 1593, as of Hurstborne, Hants (s. Robert); brother of the next-named. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Floyd, Richard, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 18, B.A. 16 Dec., 1595; M.A. from Lincoln Coll. 6 July, 1598, B.D. 18 March, 1605-6, D.D. 1617, as Lloyd, which see.
Oxenbregg, Henry, of Hants, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 16; student of Inner Temple 1594, as of Hurstborne, Hants (s. Robert), See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Watts, Mountague, of Northants, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 15; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1604; brother of Edward 1592, See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Genge, Thomas, Dorset, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2. aged 19; one of these names rector of Bradwell-by-the-sea, Essex, 1611. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Humphrey, Edward (Humfrey), of co. Merioneth, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 20; B.A. from Jesus Coll. 1 March, 1594-5.
So(u)thworth, John, of co. Lancaster, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 17.
Morgans, Peirce, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 20.
Wilton, John, of Cumberland, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 22.
Scutt, Thomas, of Dorset, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 24.
Webb, George, of London, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 17.
Day, Joseph, of London, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 13.
Day, William, of Beds, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Feb., 1591-2, aged 14.

Hanmer, John, of Salop, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 June, 1592, aged 16; fellow All Souls' Coll. 1596, B.A. 14 July, 1596, M.A. 5 April, 1600, proctor 1605, licenced to preach 23 Jan., 1614-15, B. and D.D. 1 Dec., 1615, rector of Stoke Lacy, co. Hereford, 1603, of Hutton, Somerset, 1608-15, canon of Worcester 1614, rector of Bingham, Notts, 1615-24, chaplain to James I. 1614, bishop of St. Asaph 1624, and rector of Llanfyllin, co. Montgomery, 1627, until his death at Pentrepant 23 June, 1629. See Ath. ii. 879; Lansdowne MS. 984, f. 118; Thomas, 227; D.N.B.; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Foster, Thomas, of Middlesex, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 March, 1592-3, aged 14; perhaps bar.-atlaw, Inner Temple, 1603 (as son of Thomas, of Hunsdon, Herts), and brother of Sir Robert. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Brodnax, William, of Kent, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 June, 1592, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1602, as 4th son of Thomas, of Godmersham, Kent, gent. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Andrews, James (Andros), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 30 June, 1592, aged 22; B.A. from Christ Church 5 June, 1594, M.A. from Oriel Coll. 8 July, 1597.
Cobbes, William, of Kent, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 June, 1592, aged 16; of Gray's Inn 1594, as son of Thomas, of Chilham, Kent, gent. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Sharpe, James, of Yorks, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 20 March, 1592-3, aged 16; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 7 Dec., 1597, M.A. 11 July, 1600.
Dene, Richard, of Devon, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 June, 1592, aged 17; probably son of Roger, of Newton Petrok, Devon.
Norwood, Tyrringham, of Bucks, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 16; perhaps father of Charles 1638.
Ayraye, Thomas, of co. Durham, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 7 April, 1592, aged 16; B.A. 17 Oct., 1598.
Norwoode, Edmund, of Bucks, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. entry 15 Dec., 1592, aged 14.
Catesbye, Francis, of Bucks, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 15 Dec., 1592, aged 16.

Morgan, Evan, of co. Carnarvon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 Feb., 1593-4, aged 20; B.A. 13 Feb., 1595-6; M.A. from Lincoln Coll. 3 Nov., 1598, B.D. 21 March, 1616-17; perhaps precentor of Llandaff 1599, vicar of Llanasa, Flints, 1602, of Mold, Flints, 1612, sinecure rector of Caerwys, Flints, and comportioner of Llansannan, co. Denbigh, 1614, rector of Denbigh 1614, and sinecure rector of Pennant, co. Montgomery, 1616, cursal prebendary of St. Asaph 1616, and rector of Llanferras, co. Denbigh, 1616. See Thomas, St. Asaph, & Foster's Index Eccl.

Jones, John, of co. Monmouth, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 Feb., 1593-4, aged 19; B.A. 6 July, 1596' then a student in common law; student of Inner Temple 1596, as of Hardwick, co. Monmouth (eldest son of John). See Foster's Inns of Court Register.

Hughes, Nicholas, of Herts, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 Feb., 1593-4, aged 18; B.A. 23 Feb., 1597-8, M.A. 10 July, 1601, then in orders, prebendary of St. Davids 1609, vicar of South Weald, Essex, 1611. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Dalbye, Richard, of co. Warwick, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 Feb., 1593-4, aged 18; of the Middle Temple 1596, as of New Inn, gent., son and heir of Edmund, of Butlers Marston, co. Warwick. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Haslewood, Thomas (Haselwood), of co. Worcester, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 Feb., 1593-4, aged 14; of Wickwarren, co. Worcester (s. Foulke, of Wick near Pershore), died 27 Oct., 1624; grandfather of Thomas 1669.
Chapman, Robert, of London, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 Feb., 1593-4, aged 17; a student of Gray's Inn 1595, see also next entry. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Poulton, John, of Northants, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 Feb., 1593-4, aged 17; of Desborough, Northants (s. George); died 1641. See also Ferdinando Pulton.
Bryne, John, of co. Cardigan, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 Feb., 1593-4, aged 20; called Buyard in Mat. Reg.
Loftus, William, of Yorks, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 Feb., 1593-4, aged 19; B.A. 23 Feb., 1597-8.
Porter, Ambrose, of co. Gloucester, equitis fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 Feb., 1593-4, aged 18.
Lewis, James, of co. Cardigan, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 Feb., 1593-4, aged 19.
Chambers, Thomas, of Salop, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 Feb., 1593-4, aged 18.

Barlow, Ralph, of co. Chester, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. April, 1594, aged 20; chaplain New College, B.A. 12 June, 1596, M.A. 28 April, 1599 (incorp. at Cambridge 1604), B.D. from Corpus Christi Coll. 19 June, 1606, licenced to preach 29 Oct., 1606, D.D. 11 June, 1610, rector of Radnage, Bucks, 1606, and of Farthingstone, Northants, 1609, of Fawley, Hants, 1611, etc., preb. of Lincoln 1608, archdeacon of Winchester 1609, and preb. 1611, dean of Bath and Wells 1621, buried in Wells Cathedral 27 July, 1631. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Le Neve, i. 154.

Cluet, Richard (Cluat), of Somerset, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 29 Nov., 1594, aged 15; B.A. 28 Jan., 1599-1600, M.A. 30 Oct., 1606, B. & D.D. 25 June, 1619, rector of St. Aldate's, Oxford, 1614, canon of St. Paul's 1616, rector of St. Ann and St. Agnes, London, 1617, vicar of Fulham and archdeacon of Middlesex 1620. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Rawlinson, v. 114.

Price, David, of co. Cardigan, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 12 July, 1594, aged 18; perhaps supld. for B.A. from Jesus Coll. 30 April, 1594; one of these names rector of Lawrenny, co. Pembroke, 1604. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Jackman, William (Jakeman), of co. Worcester, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 17, B.A. 20 Feb., 1600-1; probably of Berrowe, co. Worcester; father of Thomas 1643, and William 1638; brother of John 1602.
Langley, John, of Salop, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 14; perhaps B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1597-8, M.A. 1601, incorporated 14 July, 1602. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Dawnay, Darcy, of Yorks, militis fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 29 Nov., 1594, aged 19; of Gray's Inn 1596 (as son of John, of Sessay, Yorks, knight), died s.p. in 1610. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Floyd, Griffith, of co. Cardigan, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 12 July, 1594, aged 17, as Lloyd and perhaps sup. for B.A. from Jesus Coll. 7 Feb., 1596-7, as Lloyd, which see.
Lloyd, Griffith, of co. Cardigan, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 12 July, 1594, aged 17 (subs. as Floyd); perhaps sup. for B.A. from Jesus Coll. 7 Feb., 1596-7.
Smith, Robert (Smyth), of co. Lincoln, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. entry 7 June, 1594, aged 14; student of Inner Temple 1597. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
David, Griffin, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 8 Nov., 1594, aged 17 (Griffith in Mat, Reg.); B.A. 29 Jan., 1599-1600, as Davids.
Hale, George, of co. Lancaster, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 14 June, 1594, aged 18, called "Hayle" in Mat. Reg.
Pidcourt, Robert, of co. Stafford, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 29 Nov., 1594, aged 18.
Bowen, Thomas, of co. Pembroke, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 29 Nov., 1594, aged 15.

Kyffin, Thomas, of co. Carnarvon, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 17 Nov., 1595, aged 16; B.A. 15 Jan., 1598-9, M.A. 29 Oct., 1601; vicar of Whitford, Flints, 1598, of Welshpool, co. Montgomery, 1600, and of Berriew, co. Montgomery, 1605, cursal canon 1608, prebendary of St. Asaph 1614, sinecure rector of Llandyssil, co. Montgomery, 1617. See Thomas, St. Asaph, 491; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Garnons, Henry, of co. Hereford, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 27 Feb., 1595-6, aged 17; B.A. from Christ Church 24 Oct., 1601, M.A. 4 July, 1604, sup. for licence to preach 29 Nov., 1613, vicar of Mansell Gamage, co. Hereford, 1615, and St. Martin's, Hereford, 1616. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Norwood, Henry, of co. Gloucester, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 20 Feb., 1595-6, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1606, as 2nd son of William, of Leckhampton, co. Gloucester, esq. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Wargent, John, of co. Hereford, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 27 Feb., 1595-6, aged 18, B.A. 22 March, 1598-9.
Leame, John, of Sussex, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 7 Nov., 1595, aged 17.

Higford, William, of co. Gloucester, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 14 Jan., 1596-7, aged 16; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 16 Feb., 1598-9; of Dixton, near Alderton, co. Gloucester, justice of the peace (s. William); died at Dixton 6 April, 1657, aged 77. See Ath. iii. 429; Rudder's Gloucester, 220; & D.N.B.

Pigot, Alban (Pyggott), of Bucks, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 23 July, 1596, aged 17; of Marcham, Berks; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1605 (as son and heir of Francis, of Colewick, Bucks, esq.), buried in the body of the Temple church 4 Oct., 1648; father of Francis 1632. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Blague, John, born in Surrey (s. Thomas, dean of Rochester, D.D.). Oriel Coll., matric. 16 Feb., 1596-7, aged 17; (? M.A. Cambridge and incorp. 18 June, 1600, as preceeding entry). See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & O.H.S., x. 366. [Addenda: M.A. from Jesus Coll., Cambridge, 1603.]

Atwood, Edmund, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1596, aged 13; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 13 Feb., 1601-2, M.A. 17 April, 1605, rector of Staunton, co. Worcester, 1615, vicar of Hartpury, co. Gloucester, 1634. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Morgan, Edward, of co. Gloucester, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 6 Aug., 1596, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1597, as son and heir of Edward, of Adderley, co. Gloucester, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Dawtrey, Henry (Dawtrye), of Essex, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 23 July, 1596, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1607, as of Suffolk, gent., possibly knighted 27 Sept., 1630, and father of the next named.
Grover, John, of co. Leicester, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 6 Aug., 1596, aged 18; student of Middle Temple 1598, as 2nd son of John, of Norton, co. Leicester, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Greene, Thomas, of Kent, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 5 Nov., 1596, aged 15; student of Gray's Inn 1599, as of Bobbing, Kent. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Belsonn, Augustine, of Oxon, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 6 Aug., 1596, aged 17; of Aston Rowant, probably son and heir of William, of Brill, Bucks.
Marston, Richard, of Yorks, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 23 July, 1596, aged 17; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 14 June, 1600.
Cripps, Ralph, of Beds, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 16 Feb., 1596-7, aged 16; B.A. 11 Dec., 1600.
Wilson, Thomas, of Sussex, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 23 July, 1596, aged 15.
Cobbs, Thomas, of Kent, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 16 Feb., 1596-7, aged 17.

Harley, (Sir) Robert, of co. Hereford, arm. fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 April, 1597, aged 17; B.A. 12 July, 1599, a student of common law in Middle Temple 1599 (as son and heir of Thomas, of Brampton Castle, co. Hereford, esq.), K.B. 1603, master of the Mint 1626-35, 1643-9, M.P. Radnor 1604-11, Evesham, 1628-9, co. Hereford 1624-6, April-May, 1640 (k.b.), and 1640 (l.p.) till secluded 1648; died 6 Nov., 1656; father of Edward 1638. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary & D.N.B. etc.

Whetcombe, John, of Dorset, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. — Oct., 1597, aged 17, B.A. 11 July, 1599; fellow Exeter Coll. 1602-10, M.A. 17 Dec., 1602, B.D. 27 March, 1610, (incorporated at Cambridge 1610), D.D. 30 June, 1612; rector of Maiden Newton 1609-36, and of Frome Vauchurch, (both) Dorset, 1620-35; buried 30 May, 1640. See Boase, 55; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Hatton, Christopher, of Surrey, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 19; B.A. 29 Jan., 15991600, a student of Middle Temple 1601, as son and heir of Richard, of Long Ditton, Surrey, gent; (who was 3rd son of Richard, of Shrewsbury), by Mary, his wife, daughter of George Evelyn, of Long Ditton. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Babington, Henry, of Oxon, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 10 June, 1597, aged 16; son of Philip, of Kiddington, Oxon, aged 14, at the Herald's Visitation in 1595, sold his paternal estate and purchased the estate of Heaton Jesmond in Newcastle-upon-Tyne, knighted by James I. 1 May, 1617, either at Heaton or Hexham, father of William 1624.

Bodvell, John, of co. Carnarvon, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 14; B.A. 29 April, 1602, (? created D.C.L. 20 Feb., 1643-4, see next entry), a student of the Inner Temple 1602, as of Bodville, co. Carnarvon, perhaps identical with the next named. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Lloyde, Robert, of co. Montgomery, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 17 (subs. as Floyd); perhaps B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 17 July, 1601; chaplain New Coll., M.A. 29 May, 1605; one of these names beneficed in Oxfordshire. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Catcher, Edward, of London, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 April, 1597, aged 11; B.A. from Balliol Coll. 14 Feb., 1602-3, joined the Society of Jesus at Louvain in 1609 or 1611, died about 1624. See Foley's Records & D.N.B.

Savage, John, of co. Worcester, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 10 June, 1597, aged 16; student of Middle Temple 1597, as son and heir of William, of Elmley Castle, co. Worcester, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Meredith, John, of Sussex, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. — Oct., 1597, aged 17; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 1 Feb., 1599-1600; vicar of St. Peter the Great, Chichester, 1616. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Fasti, i. 281.
Floyd, Robert, of co. Montgomery, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 17, as Lloyde; perhaps B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 17 July, 1601, chaplain of New Coll., M.A. 29 May, 1605.
Childe, William, of co. Worcester, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 10 June, 1597, aged 19; of Northwicke, co. Worcester, father of William, B.C. L. 1632.
Stanley, Rowland, of co. Montgomery, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 18, B.A. 19 May, 1601.
Bagenall, Nicholas, of co. Carnarvon, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 15.
Skinner, George (Skynner), of Devon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 18.
Beveredge, Francis, of co. Derby. pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 19.
Arney, Henry, of co. Monmouth, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 15.
Quarme, Nicholas, of Devon, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 14 Oct., 1597, aged 16.

Saunders, John, of Bucks, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 26 Jan., 1598-9, aged 18; B.A. 11 Feb., 1601-2, fellow, M.A. 23 Oct., 1605 (incorporated at Cambridge 1607), B. and D.Med. and licenced to practice medicine 20 June, 1628; principal of St. Mary Hall 1632-44, and provost of Oriel 1644, until his death 20 March, 1652-3, buried in the college chapel at the high altar; son of Richard, of Long Marston, Bucks. See Gutch, i. 135; & Burrows, 536.

Sankey, Richard, of Salop, cler. fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 20 Oct., 1598, aged 16; B.A. 7 June, 1602; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 14 May, 1605; incorporated at Cambridge 1608, rector of Wem, 1606, and of Hodnet, Salop, 1615; brother of Samuel, and father of Jerome. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Sankie, Samuel, of Salop, cler. fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 20 Oct., 1598, aged 17, B.A. 7 June, 1602; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 6 June, 1605; rector of Sherfield English, Hants, 1625; brother of Richard 1598, and father of Arthur 1629, and of William 1639. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Morgans, Robert, of Flints, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 30 June, 1598, aged 17; B.A. 26 Jan., 1601-2, bar.-at-law, of Inner Temple, 1610, as son of Edward, who was of Golden Grove, Flints, and a barrister 1571. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Tomkins, Thomas, of Beds, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 26 Jan., 1598-9, aged 19; B.A. from Hart Hall 11 Feb., 1601-2, vicar of Houghton Regis, Beds, 1607. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Morgan, William, of Flints, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 30 June, 1598, aged 16; B.A. 29 April, 1602, M.A. 30 April, 1605.
Hunte, William, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 2 March, 1598-9, aged 15; B.A. 24 Jan., 1606-7.
Herbert, Edward (Harbert), of Berks, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 13 May, 1598, aged 18; B.A. 13 Feb., 1605-6.
Conway, John, of co. Flint, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 30 June, 1598, aged 15; called Canweye in Mat. Reg.
Nicholls, Thomas, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 30 June, 1598, aged 14, Nicholl in Chester.
Williames, Philip, of co. Hereford, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 Dec., 1598, aged 17.
Nicholls, John, of co. Glamorgan, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 30 June, 1598, aged 16.
Vaughan, Hugh, of co. Montgomery, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 30 June, 1598, aged 19.
Ham, John, of Devon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 13 March, 1598-9, aged 17.

Mor(r)ice, Andrew, of co. Denbigh, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 19, B.A. 11 Feb., 1601-2; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 23 Oct., 1604, chaplain All Souls' Coll.; rector of Erbistock, co. Denbigh, 1606, of Oddington-uponOtmore, Oxon, 1614, of Chiddingstone, Kent, 1623, dean of St. Asaph 1634, rector of Llanycil 1640, and comportioner of Llansannan, co. Merioneth, and vicar of Corwen, co. Denbigh, 1641; deprived during the commonwealth; died about 1654. See Thomas, 242; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Berkeley, John (Barcley), of Salop, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 17, B.A. 7 June, 1602; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 14 May, 1605, B.D. 13 May, 1618, headmaster Oswestry School 1606, rector of Llanddoget, co. Denbigh, 1606, vicar of Llansannan, co. Denbigh, 1611; rector of Newtown 1613, preb. of Llanfair in St. Asaph 1621, sinecure rector of Llandyssil, co. Montgomery, 1623, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Lloyd, (Sir) Marmaduke, of co. Pembroke, gen. fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 14, B.A. 28 June, 1603; of Milfield, co. Cardigan, bar.-at-law, of Middle Temple, 1609 (as son and heir of Thomas, of St. Davids, co. Pembroke), puisne judge court of sesssion, county palatine of Cheshire 1625, knighted 7 April, 1622. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Griffin, Henry, of co. Pembroke, cler. fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 17, B.A. 28 June, 1603; M.A. from Jesus Coll. 20 May, 1607, rector of Gumfreston, co. Pembroke, 1604, canon of St. David's 1610, rector of St. Petrox, co. Pembroke, 1614, vicar of St. Ishmael's, co. Pembroke, 1625, as Griffith. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Brynker, William (Brinker), of co. Carnarvon, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 18, B.A. 7 July, 1602; M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 2 June, 1606, rector of Llaniestyn 1613, and of Llanthaiarn 1619, and of Llan-Eigan, co. Carnarvon, 1623. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Salesburie, John, of co. Denbigh, gent. Oriel Coll. matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 18; B.A. 29 April, 1602, M.A. 25 May, 1606, then in orders; B.D. from Wadham Coll. 12 Dec., 1615; rector of Clifford Chambers, co. Gloucester, 1609. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Gardiner, 22.

Moore, John, of Devon, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 19; perhaps B.A. from St. Mary Hall 6 June, 1605, and possibly a student of Middle Temple 1602, as son and heir of Humphrey, of Cullompton, Devon, esq., deceased. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Stone, Simon, of Sussex, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 17, B.A. 9 May, 1603; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1610 (as 3s. Andrew, late of Lewes, Sussex, esq., deceased), buried in the Temple church 30 June, 1648. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Bone, Ambrose, of Devon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 18; B.A. 9 May, 1603, M.A. 25 May, 1606, then in orders; rector of Poltimore, Devon, 1607, and of Huxham 1615, father of William. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Morgans, Harry, (Henry in Mat. Reg.), of co. Glamorgan, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 13 (or 17); B.A. 9 May, 1603, M.A. 9 July, 1613, rector of St. Fagan, co. Glamorgan, 1618. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Price, James (Prise), of co. Cardigan, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 Nov., 1599, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1608, as son and heir of John Pryse, of Rhandir, co. Cardigan. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Johnes, Thomas, of co. Carnarvon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 19; B.A. 12 Nov., 1601, M.A. 7 July, 1604; one of these names beneficed in Pembrokeshire. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Turbervile, Arundel, of Dorset, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 19, B.A. 11 Feb., 1601-2; son of Nicholas, of Dorset, gent.; brother of George 1595.
Robins, William, of co. Carnarvon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 16, B.A. 9 May, 1603; M.A. from New Coll. 6 June, 1607, then in orders.
Price, Henry, of co. Merioneth, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 16; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1608. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Davies, Hugh, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 26 Oct., 1599, aged 20, B.A. 1 March, 1602-3; M.A. from Broadgates Hall 17 June, 1608.
Southworth, Thomas (Soothworth), of co. Lancaster, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 20, B.A. 22 Oct., 1602.
Prichard, Humphrey, of co. Carnarvon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 17; B.A. 10 Oct., 1601.
Salesbury, William, of co. Denbigh, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 19.
Vaughan, William, of co. Merioneth, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 18.
Jones, Griffin, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 18.
Maurice, John, of co. Montgomery, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 17.
Williams, John, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 20.
Lewys, Lewys, of co. Pembroke, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 18.
Twycrofte, Walter, of Surrey, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 17.
Hughes, Peter, of co. Flint, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 19.
Williames, Hugh, of Salop, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1599, aged 18.

Wilson, Samuel, of Beds, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1600, aged 15; B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 30 Jan., 1604-5, M.A. 13 Nov., 1611; rector of Bedford St. Martin 1621; perhaps licenced 29 Sept., 1623, to marry Sarah, daughter of Charles Harvey, deceased, at St. George, Botolph Lane. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Pigoate, Thomas, of Bucks, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. entry 24 Oct., 1600, aged 13, B.A. 5 May 1604; student of Middle Temple 1607 (as 3s. Francis, of Colwick, Bucks, esq.), buried at Nether Winchendon 28 June, 1623; brother of Albany. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Burges, Walter, of Dorset, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 12 Dec., 1600, aged 20; B.A. 23 June, 1604, M.A. 22 June, 1608, vicar of Buckland Ripers, Dorset, 1608. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Hungerforde, Anthony, of co. Gloucester, equitis fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1600, aged 15; student of Lincoln's Inn 1602; brother of John 1605. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Blencow, Robert (Blincoe), of Surrey, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1600, aged 17, B.A. from Queen's Coll. 14 June, 1604; M.A. from Oriel Coll. 21 May, 1607, then in orders.
Stewarde, Augustine, of London, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 17 Jan., 1600-1, aged 17.
Hawkins, Henry, of Wilts, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1600, aged 17.
Box, Henry, of co. Oxon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 24 Oct., 1600, aged 14.

Kinaston, (Sir) Francis, of Salop, equitis fil. nat. max. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1601, aged 14; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 14 June, 1604, M.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1609 (incorporated 11 Nov., 1611), taxor 1623, and senior proctor Cambridge 1624, "the poet," bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1611 (s. Sir Edward, of Otely, Salop), knighted 1 Jan., 1618, M.P. Salop 1621-2, "the first regent of the college or academy called the Musæum Minerva"; died in 1652. See Foster's Judges and Barristers & Ath. iii. 38.

Puleston, John, of Oxon, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 18; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1616 (as son and heir of Richard, of Worthy Abbots, Hants, gent.); created serjeant-atlaw by the parliament 1648, a justice of common pleas 1649-53; died 5 Sept., 1659; brother of Richard 1607. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Potts, John, of Beds, arm. Oriel Coll. matric. 8 May, 1601, aged 14; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1611, as son and heir of Nicholas, of Chalgrave, Beds; baptised there 12 May, 1686; brother of Crofts and of Sandes. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Tracy, Shackerley, of co. Gloucester, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 Dec., 1601, aged 15; student of Middle Temple 1602, as 3s. of Paul, of Stanway, co. Gloucester, esq.; brother of Nathan, and of Richard. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Huntley, Henry, of co. Gloucester, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 29 Jan., 1601-2, aged 14; B.A. 14 Dec., 1605; student of Middle Temple 1607, as 3rd son of James, of Elkeston, co. Gloucester, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Nevill, George, of Sussex, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 17; student of Middle Temple 1605 (as 3s. Francis, of Chichester, Sussex, esq.); brother of Richard 1603. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Mansell, Walter, of co. Carmarthen, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 13; B.A. 15 July, 1603, student of Lincoln's Inn 1605. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Ashworth, Edward, of Oxon, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 13 Nov., 1601, aged 14, B.A. 23 Jan., 1604-5; M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 20 Oct., 1608, B.Med. 10 July, 1615.
Burghill, Rowland, of co. Hereford, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 16 Oct., 1601, aged 15; (called Burrell in Mat. Reg.) B.A. 29 Oct., 1604, M.A. 17 June, 1608.
Tristram, John, of Devon, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 10 July, 1601, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1611. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Davies, Edward, of Salop, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1601, aged 17; B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 26 June, 1605, M.A. 7 Feb., 1608-9.
Powel, Thomas, of Salop, arm. fil. nat. max. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1601, aged 19; perhaps B.A. from Jesus Coll. 29 Jan., 1604-5.
Maunder, John, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 3 April, 1601, aged 20; B.A. from St. Alban Hall 4 Feb., 1604-5.
Reece, Edward, of co. Hereford, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 26 June, 1601, aged 17; B.A. 8 May, 1605, as Reese.
Price, Edward, of co. Montgomery, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 22 May, 1601, aged 16; B.A. 26 Oct., 1604.
Williams, Philip, of co. Monmouth, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. entry 11 Dec., 1601, aged 14.
Vaughan, Evan, of Salop, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 Dec., 1601, aged 18.

Sanford, Francis (Sandeford), of Salop, arm. fil. nat. max. Oriel Coll., matric. 26 Nov., 1602, aged 15, B.A. 8 June, 1605; student of Gray's Inn 1606 (as son and heir of Arthur, of Sanford, Salop, esq.); his estates were sequestered for his loyalty to the king, was allowed to compound; died in 1653, father of the next-named. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Hughes, John, of co. Cardigan, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 June, 1602, aged 20; B.A. 24 Jan., 1605-6 (M.A.), B.D. 26 Nov., 1611; D.D. from Corpus Christi Coll. 2 July, 1621, rector of Tredunnock, co. Monmouth, 1611, precentor of Llandaff 1611, vicar of Trelleck, co. Monmouth, 1611; perhaps father of Oliver. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Roberts, Theodore, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 June, 1602, aged 20; B.A. 6 Dec., 1604; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 28 May, 1611, vicar of Llandrillo in Edeirnion, 1611, and of Llanfor, co. Merioneth, 1624. See Thomas, 703; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Bradshaw, James, of co. Gloucester, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 18 Feb., 1602-3, aged 17; B.A. from Merton Coll. 19 April, 1608; M.A. from Oriel Coll. 5 July, 1611, then in orders, vicar of Chalfont St. Peter, Bucks, 1617. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Evans, Daniel, of Sussex, cler. fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 26 Nov., 1602, aged 19; B.A. 5 July, 1606, M.A. 18 May, 1609, rector of Wickham, Hants, 1615; possibly fellow and vice-principal of Jesus Coll.; died 1653. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Gutch, i. 590.

Cave, Richard, of Northants, arm. fil. nat. max. Oriel Coll., matric. 22 Oct., 1602, aged 15; B.A. 16 April, 1605 (as 'eq. fil.'), died at Padua 26 July, 1606, aged 19, pyramidal monument in Stanford Church, brother of Oliver and of Thomas 1608.

Jackeman, John, of co. Worcester, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 June, 1602, aged 19; B.A. from St. Mary Hall 14 June, 1604, M.A. 17 June, 1613, rector of Eaton Bishop, co. Hereford, 1612; brother of William 1594. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Jones, John, of co. Flint, cler. fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 26 Nov., 1602, aged 20; perhaps B.A. from Christ Church 7 July, 1608, and vicar of Eastyn (or Queenhope), Flints, 1616. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Wharton, George, of co. Durham, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 June, 1602, aged 18; of Newton Wallis, co. Durham, student of Gray's Inn 1604, as son of Christopher, of co. Durham, gent. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Blakeway, John (Blackway), of co. Hereford, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 7 May, 1602, aged 16; B.A. 21 Oct., 1605, M.A. 17 June, 1608, vicar of Stoke Bliss, co. Hereford, 1616. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Plumer, Robert, of Sussex, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1602, aged 19, B.A. 22 May, 1606; student of Lincoln's Inn 1609, as s. Robert, of Great Yeldham, Essex. See Foster's Inns of Court Register.
Williams, John, of co. Anglesea, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 26 Nov., 1602. aged 18, B.A. 11 March, 1602-3, M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 7 July, 1604, B.D. 11 July, 1611. See O.H.S. xii. 240.
Price, John, of co. Merioneth, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 June, 1602, aged 17, B.A. 22 Oct., 1602; M.A. from Hart Hall 26 June, 1605.
Kinsey, John, of Cheshire, cler. fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 June, 1602, aged 16 (subs. Kynsey); B.A. 8 May, 1605.
Cockram, George, of Devon, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 June, 1602, aged 18; B.A. 8 June, 1605, M.A. 2 July, 1612.
Edwardes, John, of Salop, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 9 July, 1602, aged 17; B.A. 4 Feb., 1607-8.
Lloyd, Evan, of co. Denbigh, equitis fil. nat, max. Oriel Coll., matric. 26 Nov., 1602, aged 16.
Wall, Richard, of Bucks, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 June, 1602, aged 18, B.A. 15 Dec., 1603.
Berry, Edward, of Berks, 'cler. fil.' Oriel Coll., matric. 15 Oct., 1602, aged 19.
Brice, Stephen (Bryce), of Oxon, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 June, 1602, aged 13.
Lloyd, David, of co. Denbigh, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 26 Nov., 1602, aged 20.

Knolles, (Sir) Robert (Knowels), of Berks. Oriel Coll., matric. 13 May, 1603, aged 15 (as arm.), of Rotherfield Greys, (s. Richard 1564), knighted 12 Jan., 1612-13, M.P. Abingdon 1614, Berks 1621-2, Abingdon 1624-6, and Wallingford 1628-9; buried 26 June, 1659; brother of Francis 1603, and father of William 1636. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Lloyde, William, of co. Merioneth, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 18 (as Floyd); B.A. from Hart Hall 11 Feb., 1606-7 (as Lloyd), rector of Trawsfynydd, co. Merioneth, 1611. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Meech, Percivall, of Dorset, cler. fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 18; B.A. 31 Oct., 1606, M.A. 20 June, 1609; rector of West Knighton, Dorset, 1609. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Knolles, Francis (Knowels), of Berks, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. entry dated 13 May, 1603, aged 11; of Stanford-in-the-Vale, Berks (s. Richard); died 4 Aug., 1640; brother of Robert 1603.
Floyd, William (Floid), of co. Merioneth, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 18, as Lloyde; and B.A. from Hart Hall 11 Feb., 1606-7, as Lloyd, which see.
Harding, Andrew, of Salop, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 15; B.A. from Hart Hall 11 Feb., 1606-7.
Thomas, Oliver, of co. Montgomery, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 16, B.A. 14 Dec., 1605.
Francklin, Richard, of Wilts, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 14; B.A. 24 Jan., 1606-7.
Roche, Richard (Rooch), of co. Gloucester, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 18.
Moulton, William, of co. Gloucester, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 23 March, 1603-4, aged 19.
Francklin, William, of Wilts, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 18.
Saunders, Michael, of Bucks, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 16.
Francklin, George, of Wilts, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 1 July, 1603, aged 15.

Brathwaite, Richard, of Northumberland, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 22 Feb., 1604-5, aged 16; (2s. Thomas, of Warcop, Westmorland), of Barnside, Westmorland, D.L., a noted wit and poet, author of 'Drunken Barnaby's Itinerary,' captain of a foot company in the trained bands, one of the projected order of the knights of the royal oak, died at East Appleton, near Richmond, Yorks, 4 May, 1673, aged 85. See Ath., iii. 986; Gray's Inn Reg., ed. Foster; & D.N.B.

Springett, (Sir) Thomas, of Sussex, gen. fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 22 Feb., 1604-5, aged 15, B.A. 4 Feb., 1607-8; of Broyle Place, Sussex, student of Middle Temple 1608 (as son and heir of Herbert, of Lewes, Sussex, gent.), knighted 22 June, 1621; died 17 Sept., 1639, aged 51, brother of Herbert, and father of the next-named, and of Anthony. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Philpotts, Thomas, of co. Hereford, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1604, aged 18; B.A. 21 June, 1608, M.A. 15 April, 1611, student of Inner Temple 1607, as of Vowchurch, co. Hereford; rector of Tibberton, co. Gloucester, 1611, and vicar of Lydney, co. Gloucester, 1623, canon of Wells 1632. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Stafford, Anthony, of Northants, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 8 March, 1604-5, aged 18, created M.A. 18 July, 1623; student Inner Temple 1606, as of Blatherwick, Northants, gent. (5s. Humphrey); died in the civil wars. See Ath. iii. 33; & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Humphreys, Robert (Humfryes), of Oxon, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 22 Feb., 1604-5, aged 15; B.A. 24 Oct., 1608, M.A. 26 June, 1611, rector of Marsh Baldon, Oxon, 1620, presented by Anne, widow of Augustin Roberts, alias Humphreys. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Pembruge, Anthony, of co. Hereford, arm. fil. nat. max. Oriel Coll., matric. 19 Oct., 1604, aged 18; B.A. 10 June, 1607; bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1615, as of Wallington, co. Hereford, gen. (s. Anthony). See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Wightwicke, Alexander, of co. Stafford, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 12 Oct., 1604, aged 17; student of Inner Temple 1610, as of Wightwicke, co. Stafford, (1s. of Francis), died 1658; father of the next. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Littleton, William (Lyttelton), of co. Worcester, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 6 July, 1604, aged 18; student of Inner Temple 1604, as of Naunton Beauchamp, co. Worcester, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Cary, Robert, of Devon, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 22 Feb., 1604-5, aged 16; of Gray's Inn on the 26th as of Alwinton, Devon. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Woodde, Thomas, of Salop, cler. fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 15 March, 1604-5, aged 19; B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 20 Feb., 1608-9, M.A. 9 July, 1612.
Fisher, William, s. (William), of Oxon, S.T.B. Oriel Coll., matric. 22 Feb., 1604-5, aged 17; B.A. 24 Jan., 1606-7.
Barker, Robert, of Bristol (city), gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 13 July, 1604, aged 15.
Hinckes, Edmond, of co. Stafford, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 12 Oct., 1604, aged 15.
Perrott, James, of co. Worcester, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 6 July, 1604, aged 17.
Proston, John, of Somerset, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 22 Feb., 1604-5, aged 17.

Dicke, John, of Somerset, 'cler. fil.' Oriel Coll., matric. 28 June, 1605, aged 21; rector of Rowberrow, Somerset, 1607-10, and of Dunkerton 1627, until his death in 1634, father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Duncombe, Thomas, of Bucks, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 31 Jan., 1605-6, aged 18; sup. for B.A. 4 Nov., 1608, bar.-at-law, Inner Temple 1618, as of Broughton, Bucks (perhaps son of Francis). See Foster's Judges and Barristers.

Pearle, Richard, of co. Hereford, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 15 Nov., 1605, aged 16; B.A. 5 July, 1609, student of Inner Temple 1610, as of Dewswall, co. Hereford, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hastings, Hercules, of Oxon, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 Oct., 1605, aged 15; perhaps 4th son of Simon of Yelford, Oxon, and of Daylesford, co. Worcester, and brother of Charles.
Danby, Christopher, of co. York, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 Oct., 1605, aged 11; possibly son of Richard, of South Cave. See Foster's Yorkshire Visitations, 263.
Starr, Thomas, of Dorset, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 Oct., 1605, aged 16, B.A. 28 Nov., 1609; vicar of Whitsbury, Hants, 1619. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Wolmer, Edward (Woolmer), of co. Warwick, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 31 Jan., 1605-6, aged 16; B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 20 April, 1611.
Wornoe, Henry, of co. Monmouth, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 14 Feb., 1605-6, aged 19; B.A. from Jesus Coll. 20 Feb., 1608-9.
Molford, Thomas, of Devon, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 15 Nov., 1605, aged 15; 3s. Thomas, of Chulmleigh, Devon.
Oborne, Maurice, of Somerset, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 31 Jan., 1605-6, aged 17; B.A. 28 Nov., 1609.
Cradocke, John, of co. Monmouth, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 14 Feb., 1605-6, aged 17.
Colly, Thomas, of co. Hereford, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 Oct., 1605, aged 19.
Pearle, John, of co. Hereford, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 15 Nov., 1605, aged 18.

Hanmer, (Sir) John, of co. Flint, militis fil. nat. max. Oriel Coll., matric. 13 March, 1606-7, aged 15; B.A. 2 July, 1608, a student of Lincoln's Inn 1610 (as son of Sir Thomas, of Hanmer, Flints, kt.), created a baronet 8 July, 1620, M. P. Flints 1624, died same year; father of Thomas 1642. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Hampson, (Sir) Thomas, of London, militis fil. Oriel Coll., matric. 21 Nov., 1606, aged 17; of Gray's Inn 1609, an ancient 1632 (s. Sir Robert), created a baronet 3 June, 1642, master of the statute office; died at Taplow 21 Aug., 1655; father of Robert and Thomas 1641. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Taylor, John, of co. Hereford, pleb. Oriel Coll., matric. 20 May, 1606, aged 16; B.A. 28 Nov., 1609, M.A. 30 June, 1614, B.D. 27 April, 1629; perhaps vicar of Northleach, co. Gloucester, 1619, or of St. Mary the Virgin, Oxford, 1622. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Springett, Harbert, of Sussex, gent. Oriel Coll., matric. 5 Dec., 1606, aged 15; of Ringmer, Sussex, student of Middle Temple 1609, as 2s. of Herbert, of Lewes, Sussex, gent.; brother of Thomas 1605. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hewett, Henry, of London, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 21 Nov., 1606, aged 17; student of Inner Temple 1608; brother of Thomas 1606. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Hewett, Thomas, of London, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 21 Nov., 1606, aged 19; student of Gray's Inn 1611; brother of Henry 1608. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Pollard, Anthony, of Oxon, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 11 July, 1606, aged 17; B.A. 28 Nov., 1609, M.A. 9 July, 1612. See Lewis 1601.
Molford, Gilbert, of Devon, arm. Oriel Coll., matric. 4 July, 1606, aged 17; 4s. Thomas, of Chulmleigh, Devon.

Lloyd, Hugh, of co. Cardigan; servitor of Oriel Coll. about 1607, B.A. 12 Nov., 1611, M.A. 30 June, 1614, then in orders, fellow Jesus Coll., B.D. 1 July, 1624, D.D. 26 June, 1638, sinecure rector of Denbigh 1637, and rector of Hirnant, co. Montgomery, 1638 until sequestered, reinstated at the restoration, canon and archdeacon of St. Davids 1644, rector of St. Andrew 1617, and St. Nicholas, in Glamorganshire, 1626, rector of Llangattock, co. Brecon, 1661, prebendary and bishop of Llandaff 1660 until his death 7 June, 1667. See Ath. iv. 834, iii. 336n.; Foster's Index Eccl.; & Thomas, 740.
